examples.title: User Authentication with Password Hashing
examples.description: Demonstrates CONFIDENTIALITY and AUTHENTICATION functional requirements using secure password hashing with salt
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates CONFIDENTIALITY (protecting passwords through hashing) and AUTHENTICATION (verifying user identity). The system uses cryptographic salt to prevent rainbow table attacks and constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks. The register_user function creates a new identity, while authenticate_user verifies credentials.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is password length

examples.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) System
examples.description: Demonstrates AUTHORIZATION functional requirement with role-based permissions
examples.code:
from enum import Enum
from functools import wraps

# Define roles and permissions
class Role(Enum):
    ADMIN = "admin"
    MANAGER = "manager"
    USER = "user"
    GUEST = "guest"

class Permission(Enum):
    READ = "read"
    WRITE = "write"
    DELETE = "delete"
    ADMINISTER = "administer"

# Permission matrix - defines who can do what
PERMISSION_MATRIX = {
    Role.ADMIN: [Permission.READ, Permission.WRITE, Permission.DELETE, Permission.ADMINISTER],
    Role.MANAGER: [Permission.READ, Permission.WRITE, Permission.DELETE],
    Role.USER: [Permission.READ, Permission.WRITE],
    Role.GUEST: [Permission.READ]
}

class AccessControlSystem:
    """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Authorization - Role-Based Access Control"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.users = {}
    
    def add_user(self, username, role):
        """Assign role to user - FUNCTIONAL REQ: User Management"""
        self.users[username] = role
        return f"User {username} assigned role {role.value}"
    
    def check_permission(self, username, permission):
        """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Authorization - Permission verification"""
        if username not in self.users:
            return False
        
        user_role = self.users[username]
        allowed_permissions = PERMISSION_MATRIX.get(user_role, [])
        return permission in allowed_permissions
    
    def authorize(self, username, permission):
        """Decorate functions to enforce authorization"""
        def decorator(func):
            @wraps(func)
            def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
                if not self.check_permission(username, permission):
                    raise PermissionError(f"User {username} lacks {permission.value} permission")
                return func(*args, **kwargs)
            return wrapper
        return decorator

# Demonstration
print("=== Security Functional Requirement: Authorization (RBAC) ===")
acs = AccessControlSystem()

# Add users with different roles
print(acs.add_user("admin_user", Role.ADMIN))
print(acs.add_user("regular_user", Role.USER))
print(acs.add_user("guest_user", Role.GUEST))

# Check permissions
print(f"\nadmin_user can DELETE: {acs.check_permission('admin_user', Permission.DELETE)}")
print(f"regular_user can DELETE: {acs.check_permission('regular_user', Permission.DELETE)}")
print(f"guest_user can WRITE: {acs.check_permission('guest_user', Permission.WRITE)}")
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates the AUTHORIZATION functional requirement through Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). The PERMISSION_MATRIX defines what each role can do (principle of least privilege). The check_permission method enforces authorization before allowing operations. This satisfies the security functional requirement of determining WHAT an authenticated user can access and modify.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of users

examples.title: Security Audit Trail with Integrity Verification
examples.description: Demonstrates AUDIT/LOGGING and INTEGRITY functional requirements using cryptographic signatures
examples.code:
import hashlib
import json
import time
from datetime import datetime

class SecurityAuditLogger:
    """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Audit Logging - Track security-relevant events"""
    
    def __init__(self, secret_key):
        self.secret_key = secret_key
        self.audit_log = []
    
    def compute_checksum(self, data):
        """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Integrity - Verify log entry hasn't been tampered"""
        data_str = json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True)
        return hashlib.sha256((data_str + self.secret_key).encode()).hexdigest()
    
    def log_event(self, username, event_type, resource, action_result):
        """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Audit - Record security events with non-repudiation"""
        timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
        event_data = {
            "timestamp": timestamp,
            "username": username,
            "event_type": event_type,
            "resource": resource,
            "result": action_result
        }
        event_data["checksum"] = self.compute_checksum(event_data)
        self.audit_log.append(event_data)
        return event_data
    
    def verify_log_integrity(self, index):
        """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Integrity - Detect if log entries were modified"""
        if index >= len(self.audit_log):
            return False
        
        entry = self.audit_log[index]
        stored_checksum = entry.pop("checksum")
        computed_checksum = self.compute_checksum(entry)
        entry["checksum"] = stored_checksum  # Restore
        
        return secrets.compare_digest(stored_checksum, computed_checksum)
    
    def detect_tampering(self):
        """FUNCTIONAL REQ: Audit - Verify entire log chain integrity"""
        tampered_entries = []
        for i, entry in enumerate(self.audit_log):
            if not self.verify_log_integrity(i):
                tampered_entries.append(i)
        return tampered_entries

# Demonstration
import secrets

print("=== Security Functional Requirements: Audit & Integrity ===")
audit = SecurityAuditLogger(secret_key="super_secret_key_123")

# Log security events
print("Logging security events...")
log1 = audit.log_event("alice", "LOGIN", "/app", "SUCCESS")
log2 = audit.log_event("alice", "READ", "/data/records", "SUCCESS")
log3 = audit.log_event("bob", "DELETE", "/data/records", "DENIED - Insufficient permissions")
log4 = audit.log_event("admin", "ADMIN", "/system/config", "SUCCESS")

print(f"\nLogged {len(audit.audit_log)} events")
print("\nSample log entry:")
print(json.dumps(log1, indent=2))

# Verify integrity
print(f"\nLog entry 0 integrity: {audit.verify_log_integrity(0)}")
print(f"Log entry 1 integrity: {audit.verify_log_integrity(1)}")

# Simulate tampering attempt
print("\n--- Simulating tampering attack ---")
audit.audit_log[1]["result"] = "MODIFIED_BY_ATTACKER"
print(f"Tampered entry 1 integrity: {audit.verify_log_integrity(1)}")
print(f"Tampered entries detected: {audit.detect_tampering()}")
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates three critical security functional requirements: (1) AUDIT/LOGGING - recording all security-relevant events with timestamps, users, and outcomes for forensic analysis; (2) INTEGRITY - using cryptographic checksums to detect if log entries have been modified; (3) NON-REPUDIATION - ensuring users cannot deny their actions since logs are cryptographically signed. The verify_log_integrity function detects any tampering attempts, satisfying the requirement to maintain evidence of all security events.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for integrity verification of n log entries
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing n audit log entries
